justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Manual restart requirement identified as operational gap with clear language ('every DB failover = customer-facing outage until an operator manually restarts'), (2) Dynamic primary resolution recommended (DNS-based endpoint resolution, PgBouncer, RDS Proxy), (3) No retry on connection error identified as 'the single biggest resilience gap' with MUST FIX priority, (4) Read replica routing identified appropriately — framed as wasting capacity rather than as an absolute blocker, (5) Verdict is NOT READY, (6) Multiple specific recommendations provided across failure modes and escalations.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough failure mode analysis — covers not just the required gaps but also pool exhaustion, slow queries, and circuit breaker absence
Clear MUST FIX labeling on the two critical issues (manual restart, no retry) distinguishes them from optimizations
Read replica routing correctly framed as capacity/blast-radius concern rather than a hard blocker, avoiding the failure mode of treating it as a must-have
Escalations are well-structured with actionable detail — each includes the issue, why it matters, and what kind of change is needed
Confidence level of 55 is honest and well-calibrated given the unknowns about QPS, upstream retry behavior, and pool sizing
On-call documentation gap flagged with specific runbook items — goes beyond the rubric requirements

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Manual restart requirement identified as 'broken' with explicit callout that downtime is proportional to human response time. (2) Dynamic primary resolution recommended via RDS Proxy, PgBouncer, or DNS-based failover endpoint. (3) No retry on connection error identified as CRITICAL reliability gap with specific retry parameters suggested (1-2 retries, 100-500ms backoff). (4) Read replica routing identified as a risk/optimization ('wasting available capacity') rather than a hard blocker. (5) Verdict is NOT READY. (6) Multiple specific recommendations provided across failover, retry, pool exhaustion, and observability.

observations.text: Correctly categorized the baked-in connection string as 'broken' (not just 'risk'), distinguishing it from the read replica issue which was appropriately labeled 'risk'
Escalations are well-structured — both critical issues routed to Coach K with clear reasoning about why they cannot be solved by application code alone
Goes beyond the rubric with useful additional findings: connection pool exhaustion scenario, missing pool metrics, absence of circuit breakers, and missing on-call runbooks
Confidence section is honest and well-calibrated at 60% — correctly identifies what it can and cannot assess without more information
Read replica routing correctly framed as optimization ('halve primary pool pressure') rather than a reliability blocker, avoiding the failure mode of treating it as must-have
Health check analysis adds value — identifies that /health will correctly fail but nothing auto-recovers, showing understanding of the full failover cascade

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Manual restart requirement identified as critical operational gap with clear explanation of downtime impact, (2) DNS-based/proxy dynamic primary resolution recommended (PgBouncer, RDS Proxy, Consul DNS), (3) No retry on connection error identified as critical reliability gap with specific recommendation (1-2 retries, 100-500ms backoff), (4) Read replica routing identified and appropriately framed as scalability/optimization concern rather than pure reliability blocker, (5) Verdict is NOT READY, (6) Multiple specific recommendations provided across all failure modes.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough analysis — identified all three key gaps (manual restart, no retry, no read replica routing) and correctly prioritized them
Manual restart gap correctly escalated as infrastructure_gap with clear explanation of why it causes extended downtime
Retry recommendation is specific and well-calibrated: 1-2 retries, 100-500ms backoff, correctly notes read queries are safe to retry
Dynamic primary resolution recommendation includes multiple concrete options: PgBouncer, RDS Proxy, Consul-backed DNS, and test-on-borrow as minimum

Went beyond rubric requirements with valuable additional findings: connection pool exhaustion risk, slow query cascade, missing statement_timeout, circuit breaker absence, and graceful degradation gaps

Read replica routing escalated with language suggesting it is essential/critical rather than clearly framing it as an optimization — rubric notes it should not be treated as a must-have reliability blocker. However, the resilience_assessment does frame it as 'highest-impact scalability improvement' which is the correct framing.
Observability review is comprehensive with actionable gaps identified (pool utilization metrics, read/write breakdown, distributed tracing)
Confidence section is well-calibrated at 55% with honest assumptions and clear separation of high vs low confidence areas
